parsed_review.summary: CP00-916 is a descriptor-only RP30 P02 bridge (RP30.P02.M08.S08-RP30.P02.M09.S05) carrying the AttendanceWorkflow tail into RecruitmentWorkflow re-entry rows. The pack-level descriptor-only claim is fully supported by source inspection. registry.js:4242 (createHrxCp916AttendanceRecruitmentWorkflowBridgeDescriptor) and registry.js:1495-1625 (createHrxDomainRows) build deep-frozen evidence rows where every attendance/recruitment runtime, decision-write, record-write, candidate-payload, and offer-creation flag is false; validators.js:3336-3465 enforces all of these plus no-real-data, no-secret, no-separate-product, Claude-not-final-approver, and Hermes-no-runtime-receipt boundaries. Coverage matches baseline exactly: M08x5 (S08-S12) + M09x5 (S01-S05) = 10 units; deliverable {implementation:10}; micro_title {AttendanceWorkflow:5, RecruitmentWorkflow:5} (registry.js:1395-1426). All 6 commands in command-evidence.json exit 0 and every referenced npm script exists in package.json; a full scan of packages/hrx/src found no real I/O (writeFile/exec/fetch/http/process.env), structurally confirming the no-runtime/no-write attestation. Tests at model.test.js:1066-1140 assert coverage, descriptor closure, and contract projection; the contract (contracts/hrx-people-contract.json) carries latest_pack/current_pack CP00-916, latest_projection HrxCp916AttendanceRecruitmentWorkflowBridgeDescriptor, and validation.valid:true with a clean no_write_attestation. Authority boundary is intact (claude_is_final_approval:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true) and Hermes is used only as a deterministic evidence packet (emits_runtime_receipt:false). The pack correctly reports production_ready:false / pending_review with no premature claim, readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON), and no prior invalid review attempts are laundered as valid evidence. Unrelated dirty files (UI control-room, workbook docs, LDIP result) are correctly excluded per changed-file-scope.json. No P0/P1/P2 issues. One P3 informational note records that the full deterministic validation ladder and adjudication remain correctly gated after this review under non-Claude authority. This review does not block pack or CP-goal closeout.

findings.title: production_ready promotion correctly remains gated on post-review deterministic ladder and adjudication
findings.details: The pack honestly reports production_ready:false and status in_progress/pending_review (manifest.json:5-6, claude-review-result.json:4, construction-inspection.json:5-9, adjudication.md). command-evidence.json:50-52 documents that only the targeted HRX checks (syntax, model test, rp30/hrx requirement/weighted validate) have run and exit 0, while the full deterministic validation ladder (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) is intentionally deferred until review normalization and adjudication. This is the expected sequencing, not a defect: Claude is a read-only independent reviewer and not the final approver (review-request.json:8-13, descriptor authority_boundary at registry.js:4249-4254).
findings.recommendation: After this PASS review is recorded, the non-Claude (Codex/human) authority must run the full deterministic validation ladder, complete adjudication and queue regeneration, and only then flip production_ready and the hrx_attendance_recruitment_workflow_tail_bridge_descriptor_verified flag. No code change required.
findings.post_review_disposition: informational_only_non_blocking; expected post-review sequencing confirmed; does not block pack or goal closeout
